title: fix(image): respect user-provided width/height by dropping gen-side pre-scaling
author: Circuit-Overtime

gist.headline: Pixel perfect
gist.blurb: We fixed a sneaky bug that was silently upscaling smaller image requests behind the scenes. Now, when you ask for specific dimensions, you get exactly what you asked for—down to the last pixel.
gist.summary: Removed a legacy pre-scaling step in the image generation pipeline that was silently upscaling requests under 1 megapixel.
gist.impact: Image generation requests will now strictly respect the exact width and height parameters provided by the user.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot measuring a picture frame with a tape measure, emphasizing exact dimensions and precision.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, using a measuring tape to measure a perfectly sized glowing picture frame. A chunky Monitor Robot stands nearby giving a thumbs up. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Text in pixel font: 'EXACT SIZE'.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, using a measuring tape to measure a perfectly sized glowing picture frame. A chunky Monitor Robot stands nearby giving a thumbs up. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
